page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-288 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 288 vCPUs and 576 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor running at 2.3 GHz, this instance allocates 144 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2.0 threads per core. The x86_64 architecture is supported by a massive 504 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results place this instance in the top tier for multi-core CPU performance, passmark CPU score, and memory bandwidth across both cached and uncached read/write operations. It demonstrates exceptional throughput in data-heavy operations, achieving top-tier ratings in Redis SET operations, static web serving throughput, and software compilation. Advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX (Advanced Matrix Extensions) are fully supported, driving top-tier performance in cryptographic and physics simulations.

With a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, this instance is highly cost-efficient for compute-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. However, a notable tradeoff is observed in LLM text generation for small models, which registers in the bottom 25%, though prompt processing and larger model inference remain strong. The instance is highly suited for high-performance computing, large-scale software compilation, web serving, and parallelized CPU-bound data processing.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-288 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance configured with 288 vCPUs and 576 GB of RAM, driven by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor at 2.3 GHz. Featuring 144 physical cores with hyper-threading, a 504 MB L3 cache, and advanced instruction support including Intel AMX and AVX-512, this instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, making it highly specialized for raw computational density. Benchmarks show top-tier results in software compilation, database operations, and cryptography, though LLM text generation for small models is a performance bottleneck. This instance is highly cost-efficient for compute-heavy, memory-lean workloads, making it ideal for high-performance computing, parallel processing, and large-scale web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-288 is a compute-optimized instance with 288 vCPUs, 576 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for parallel workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-288 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 288 vCPUs, 576 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core CPU performance.
tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized instance delivering top-tier multi-core performance and advanced matrix extensions for parallelized CPU-bound workloads.
bullet_points: Features 288 dedicated vCPUs and 576 GB of RAM on Intel Xeon 6985P-C processors
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated cryptography and matrix math
Delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and high memory bandwidth
Lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, optimizing cost for pure compute workloads
Best suited for software compilation, high-performance computing, and parallel data processing
